label: yes
cot_prefix: Okay, let me try to figure this out. The question is about a search group formed by American scientists in 1975. I need to find out what they did according to the text. First, I'll recall the relevant part of the text. In 1975, some American scientists formed a search group. They used an underwater camera that took pictures every seventy seconds. The text says that some of the pictures seemed to show a red-brown creature with a four-meter body and a head on a four-meter neck. This made many people believe in the monster again, but even today there's no certainty. Looking at the options: A) found the monster itself. But the text says they found pictures that seemed to show a creature, not the actual monster. B) found a huge cave underwater. Wait, the text mentions that earlier (before 1975), when they used underwater cameras, they found a cave but no proof of the monster. So B is something that happened earlier, not in 1975 with the American scientists. C) believed there wasn't any monster. The text says
